symbols: cruciform metal framework — the cross abstracted into geometric form
circle at top — the head of Christ or the halo, reduced to pure geometry
red and white panels — the blood and purity associated with the crucifixion
open framework — the transparency and universality of the cross's meaning
description: A modernist outdoor sculpture consisting of a black metal framework in a roughly cruciform shape standing on a low plinth on a green lawn. A large yellow circle sits at the upper center of the framework. Below it, rectangular white and red panels fill part of the lower structure. The geometric, grid-like composition references both the cross and the De Stijl aesthetic. Behind the sculpture, a large coniferous tree and a brick wall are visible in a misty garden setting.
theological_description: This abstract sculpture takes the title 'Construction (Crucifixion): Homage to Mondrian,' placing the central symbol of Christianity — the cross — into dialogue with modernist geometric abstraction. The cruciform shape, stripped of all figurative detail, distills the cross to its essential structural form. Even in abstraction, the cross retains its theological power as the instrument of salvation. Paul wrote that 'the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God' (1 Corinthians 1:18). The reduction to primary colors and pure geometry suggests that the cross transcends any particular artistic tradition — its meaning is universal and elemental. The open framework allows the landscape to be seen through the cross, suggesting that all of creation is viewed through the lens of Christ's sacrifice.

scripture_refs.reason: The message of the cross — even abstracted into pure geometry, the cruciform shape proclaims the power of God
Boasting in nothing except the cross of Christ — the cross as the defining symbol of Christian faith
